metadata.name: reserve_sentinel
metadata.description: On-chain risk scores for tokenized RWA stocks on Solana

instructions.name: initialize_asset
docs: Create the canonical score PDA for a mint and set its metadata.
Admin-only in practice (whoever pays); called once per tracked mint.

accounts.name: mint
docs: The tokenized-stock mint this score account tracks.
Typed as `UncheckedAccount` on purpose: we do NOT deserialize it as an
SPL/Token-2022 mint here because tokenized stocks live behind several
token programs and we only need its pubkey as a PDA seed. The indexer
verifies the mint actually exists on mainnet before ever calling this.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays rent and signs the init. The project admin.

instructions.name: update_score
docs: Push fresh risk signals. Authority-gated; reads are free.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal `asset_score.authority` (enforced by `has_one`).

errors.name: UnauthorizedAuthority
errors.msg: Signer is not the registered authority for this asset.

errors.name: InvalidTicker
errors.msg: Underlying ticker must be ASCII uppercase/space, right-padded to 8 bytes.

types.name: AssetScore
docs: One `AssetScore` PDA exists per tracked mint.
PDA seeds: [b"asset_score", mint.as_ref()]
The account is a *storage + authority* layer only. All scoring math runs
off-chain in the Go indexer; the program simply guarantees that:
- there is exactly one canonical account per mint (PDA uniqueness), and
- only the registered `authority` can mutate the score fields, while
anyone (dashboards, DeFi protocols) can read them for free.
`#[derive(InitSpace)]` lets Anchor compute the byte size at compile time so
we never hand-count and get rent wrong. See `AssetScore::INIT_SPACE`.

fields.name: mint
docs: The tokenized-stock mint this score describes.

fields.name: issuer
docs: Issuer enum: 0=xStocks, 1=Ondo, 2=Backpack/Sunrise, 3=other.
Kept as a raw u8 (not a Rust enum) so new issuers can be added without
a program upgrade — the frontend owns the label mapping.

fields.name: underlying_ticker
docs: Right-padded ASCII ticker of the *underlying* equity, e.g. b"CRCL    ".
This groups cross-issuer wrappers (CRCLx vs CRCLON both -> "CRCL")
so the frontend can render side-by-side comparisons.

fields.name: trust_tier
docs: Manually assigned custodial-trust tier, 0-3:
3 = redeemable / custodial (real shares held, redeemable)
0 = purely synthetic
Feeds the `trust_tier_component` of the composite score off-chain.

fields.name: score
docs: Composite risk score, 0-100. Higher = safer.

fields.name: premium_bps
docs: Signed premium/discount vs the reference (Pyth) price, in basis points.
Positive = trading above the real stock, negative = below.

fields.name: liquidity_depth_usd
docs: Estimated USD notional required to move the on-chain price 1%.
A proxy for how thin the DEX liquidity is.

fields.name: mint_burn_z
docs: Z-score of recent mint/burn velocity, scaled x100 (so 2.5σ -> 250).
A spike signals procurement stress (issuer minting fast to meet demand).

fields.name: last_updated
docs: Unix timestamp (seconds) of the last successful `update_score`.

fields.name: authority
docs: The only key permitted to call `update_score`. Typically the indexer's
backend hot keypair. Reads are permissionless.

fields.name: bump
docs: Stored PDA bump, so downstream CPIs / re-derivations are cheap and
don't have to brute-force the canonical bump again.
